body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 16/2026
Made under: Residential Tenancy Act
Published in: No. 3 – B.C. Regs. 12 to 17, February 24, 2026
Deposit date: February 20, 2026

B.C. Reg. 16/2026, deposited February 20, 2026, under theRESIDENTIAL TENANCY ACT[section 97]. Order in Council 47/2026, approved and ordered February 20, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that, effective February 23, 2026, the Residential Tenancy Regulation, B.C. Reg. 477/2003, is amended as set out in the attached Appendix. — C. BOYLE,Minister of Housing and Municipal Affairs; R. NEILL,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	The Residential Tenancy Regulation, B.C. Reg. 477/2003, is amended by adding the following section: Exemption from Act for assisted living rental units 1.3(1)	In this section"assisted living residence"and"resident"have the same meaning as in section 1 of theCommunity Care and Assisted Living Act. (2)	Subject to subsection (3), the Act does not apply to a rental unit in an assisted living residence that is occupied by a resident. (3)	Despite subsection (2), the Act continues to apply in relation to an application for dispute resolution that has been or may be made if the dispute relates to a matter that arose before February 23, 2026. minutes_briefs.title: Assisted Living Rental Units Removed from BC Residential Tenancy Act Protection
minutes_briefs.summary: As of February 23, 2026, rental units inside assisted living residences (housing where personal care services are provided to residents) are no longer covered by BC's Residential Tenancy Act. This means the standard tenant protections — such as rules on rent increases, eviction notices, and security deposits — no longer apply to those units.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you operate or own rental units inside an assisted living residence, stop applying Residential Tenancy Act rules to new and existing tenancies immediately.

key_dates.label: BC assisted living rental unit exemption from Residential Tenancy Act takes effect
